description: Cyprus IP SPV Ltd — Cyprus — Royalty — REJECTED (conduit leg 1: immediate recipient fails BO)
legal_notes: Conduit scenario, leg 1 of 2. Cyprus treaty: MLI PPT applies; Art. 8 MLI reserved. Treaty royalty rate: 5%. SPV has zero substance — registered address only. BO condition (iii) — genuine business activity — is not met. BO condition (ii) — no pass-on obligation — is violated: the SPV has a contractual back-to-back licence agreement obliging it to remit royalties to the Israeli ultimate parent. Expected: REJECTED. Conduit risk: true (Cyprus is a known routing jurisdiction, entity type = shell_company). See case_08b for ultimate BO assessment.

input.substance_notes: Cyprus shell company. Registered address in Nicosia via a corporate services provider. Zero employees. IP registered in Cyprus but no management, R&D, or exploitation decisions made locally. Back-to-back licence: Cyprus entity sub-licenses IP from Israeli parent and licenses to Polish payer. All royalties received from Poland are contractually passed to Israeli parent under the back-to-back agreement. No economic substance in Cyprus.

expected.eval_note: Conduit leg 1. Domestic rate 20% applies. conduit_risk must be true. Eval harness links to case_08b for ultimate BO assessment.
